description_long: This object is a large solar energy infrastructure installation, featuring a prominent array of solar panels mounted on a robust structural frame. The panels are arranged in a single, continuous sloped surface, supported by a series of triangular trusses and beams, likely made of metal. The base is a wide, rectangular platform, possibly simulating a rooftop or ground foundation, with a grid-like texture. The structure includes side supports and cross-bracing for stability, and the solar panels themselves have a grid pattern typical of photovoltaic cells. The overall design suggests a fixed, ground-mounted or roof-mounted solar array intended for large-scale energy generation in an urban environment. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are apparent, and the installation appears to be static and non-movable.
description: A large, sloped solar panel array mounted on a sturdy metal frame atop a rectangular platform, designed for urban solar energy generation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sloped solar panel surface and supporting trusses, with the platform base visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the solar array and the triangular support structure beneath the panels.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the back of the solar panels and the supporting frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the open triangular trusses and the full span of the solar panel array.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with triangular steel or aluminum truss frame
attribute_array_configuration: single row of large panels forming a continuous sloped surface
